Header file for memory allocation in JerryScript, defining compressed pointers and memory management functions.

# Purpose
The code is a C header file that defines an interface for memory allocation in the JerryScript engine. It provides mechanisms for handling compressed pointers, which are used to efficiently manage memory in constrained environments. The file includes definitions for compressed pointer types, such as `jmem_cpointer_t`, which can be either 16-bit or 32-bit depending on the system configuration. It also defines macros for setting and getting compressed pointers, ensuring that memory operations are performed correctly and efficiently.

The header file includes several external function declarations for initializing and finalizing the memory management system ([`jmem_init`](<#jmem_init>) and [`jmem_finalize`](<#jmem_finalize>)), as well as for compressing and decompressing pointers ([`jmem_compress_pointer`](<#jmem_compress_pointer>) and [`jmem_decompress_pointer`](<#jmem_decompress_pointer>)). It also provides functionality for managing memory callbacks, allowing the registration and unregistration of functions that handle memory freeing events. Additionally, the file defines an enumeration for the severity of memory freeing requests, which helps manage memory usage dynamically. The inclusion of conditional compilation directives, such as `#ifdef JMEM_STATS`, indicates that the file can optionally support memory statistics tracking, providing functions to reset and print memory usage statistics.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `jrt.h`
- `jmem-config.h`
- `jmem-heap.h`
- `jmem-poolman.h`


# Data Structures

---
### jmem\_free\_unused\_memory\_severity\_t
- **Type**: ``enum``
- **Members**:
    - `JMEM_FREE_UNUSED_MEMORY_SEVERITY_LOW`: Represents a 'low' severity level for freeing unused memory.
    - `JMEM_FREE_UNUSED_MEMORY_SEVERITY_HIGH`: Represents a 'high' severity level for freeing unused memory.
- **Description**: Defines the severity levels for requests to free unused memory, with levels ranging from 'low' to 'high'. These requests are processed sequentially, starting from 'low' to 'high', until enough memory is freed. If the 'high' severity request does not free enough memory, the system shuts down with an out-of-memory error.


---
### jmem\_heap\_free\_t
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - `next_offset`: Offset of the next region in the list.
    - `size`: Size of the region.
- **Description**: Represents a node in a free memory region list, where `next_offset` indicates the position of the next free region and `size` specifies the size of the current free region.


---
### jmem\_pools\_chunk\_t
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``next_p``: Pointer to the next pool chunk in the linked list.
- **Description**: Represents a node in a linked list of memory pool chunks, where each node contains a pointer to the next chunk, facilitating the management of memory allocation in a pool-based system.

### jmem\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:jmem_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/jmem/jmem-allocator.h#L154>)

Initializes the memory management system.
- **Description**: Use this function to initialize the memory management system before performing any memory allocation operations. It must be called once at the start of the program to set up the necessary memory structures. Ensure that this function is called before any other memory management functions to avoid undefined behavior.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`jmem_init`](<jmem-allocator.c.md#jmem_init>)  (Implementation)

### jmem\_finalize<!-- {{#callable_declaration:jmem_finalize}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/jmem/jmem-allocator.h#L155>)

Finalizes the memory management system.
- **Description**: Use this function to clean up and release resources used by the memory management system. It should be called when memory management is no longer needed, typically at the end of a program's execution. This function ensures that all memory pools and heaps are properly finalized. If memory statistics are enabled and initialized, it will also print memory usage statistics. Ensure that this function is called only after all memory allocations are complete and no further memory operations are required.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`jmem_finalize`](<jmem-allocator.c.md#jmem_finalize>)  (Implementation)


---
### jmem\_register\_free\_unused\_memory\_callback<!-- {{#callable_declaration:jmem_register_free_unused_memory_callback}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/jmem/jmem-allocator.h#L160>)

Registers a callback for freeing unused memory.
- **Description**: Use this function to set a callback that the system will call when it needs to free unused memory. This function must be called only once, as it supports only one callback at a time. Ensure that no callback is currently registered before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `callback`: A function pointer of type `jmem_free_unused_memory_callback_t`. This callback will be invoked to free unused memory. The caller must ensure that this parameter is not null and that it points to a valid function.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`jmem_register_free_unused_memory_callback`](<jmem-allocator.c.md#jmem_register_free_unused_memory_callback>)  (Implementation)


---
### jmem\_unregister\_free\_unused\_memory\_callback<!-- {{#callable_declaration:jmem_unregister_free_unused_memory_callback}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/jmem/jmem-allocator.h#L161>)

Unregisters a previously registered memory free callback.
- **Description**: Use this function to remove a callback that was previously registered to free unused memory. This function must be called with the same callback that was registered earlier. It is important to ensure that the callback is currently registered, as the function will assert if the callback does not match the registered one. This function is typically used when the callback is no longer needed or before shutting down the system to clean up resources.
- **Inputs**:
    - `callback`: A function pointer of type `jmem_free_unused_memory_callback_t` that was previously registered. The callback must match the currently registered callback, otherwise the function will assert. The caller retains ownership of the callback.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`jmem_unregister_free_unused_memory_callback`](<jmem-allocator.c.md#jmem_unregister_free_unused_memory_callback>)  (Implementation)


---
### jmem\_stats\_reset\_peak<!-- {{#callable_declaration:jmem_stats_reset_peak}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/jmem/jmem-allocator.h#L164>)

Resets peak memory usage statistics.
- **Description**: Use this function to reset the recorded peak memory usage statistics for both the heap and memory pools. This is useful for monitoring memory usage over specific periods or after certain operations. Ensure that memory statistics collection is enabled in the build configuration before calling this function.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`jmem_stats_reset_peak`](<jmem-allocator.c.md#jmem_stats_reset_peak>)  (Implementation)


---
### jmem\_stats\_print<!-- {{#callable_declaration:jmem_stats_print}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/jmem/jmem-allocator.h#L165>)

Prints memory allocation statistics.
- **Description**: Use this function to output current memory allocation statistics for diagnostic or monitoring purposes. It provides information about the memory heap and memory pools. This function is available only if the `JMEM_STATS` macro is defined, indicating that memory statistics collection is enabled. Ensure that the system is configured to collect memory statistics before calling this function.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`jmem_stats_print`](<jmem-allocator.c.md#jmem_stats_print>)  (Implementation)
